content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
September 5,
1995
Nancie Ryan
Assistant General Counsel
East Bay Municipal Utility District
375 Eleventh Street
Post Office Box 24055
Oakland, California
94623-1055
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-266
Dear Ms.
Ryan:
This letter responds to your August 8,
1995, request for
assistance interpreting the disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
QUESTIONS
1.
Is the reimbursement of Director Mellon’s reasonable
travel expenses incurred to attend meetings of the Teamsters
Benefit Trust reportable income?
2.
Is the reimbursement of Director Mellon’s reasonable
travel expenses incurred to attend meetings of the Board of
Trustees of Health Care Employees/Employer Dental Trust reportable
income?
3.
Is the reimbursement of Director Mellon’s reasonable
travel expenses and tuition incurred to attend educational
seminars, reportable income?
4.
I-f any of the above are reportable income, is the
reimbursement subject to the Act’s prohibitions on the receipt of
gifts?
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18995.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—95—266
Page
2
ANSWER
The reimbursement for travel and tuition Director Mellon
receives as a trustee of the private trusts identified in your
facts would qualify as reportable income under the Act.
Director
Mellon appears to be providing equal or greater consideration in
the form of his services as a trustee in exchange for the travel
and tuition reimbursement.
Therefore, the Act’s gift limits do
not apply to these payments.
FACTS
East Bay Municipal Utility District is a publicly owned
utility providing water and wastewater treatment services to
portions of Alameda and Contra Costa Counties.
Frank Mellon is a
director of the East Bay Municipal Utility District.
Director Mellon is employed- by the firm of McDowell Conhain
Associates in San Mateo, California.
The firm provides employee
relations consulting services to union and nonunion companies.
In
addition, Director Mellon serves without compensation as a trustee
and fiduciary for four Taft-Hartley trusts which provide pension,
health, dental,
and educational benefits for covered employees and
their dependents.
A Taft-Hartley trust is a private trust
governed by section 302 of the Taft-Hartley Act and the Employee
Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.
In fulfillment of his private trust duties, Director Mellon
travels to trust board meetings which are held in Tahoe, Monterey
and Yosemite.
Director Mellon is reimbursed for his travel and
accommodations on these trips.
Director Mellon also attends
continuing education conferences at the expense of the private
trusts.
Expenses which are not reimbursed by the private trusts
are deducted as business expenses by Director Mellon.
One of the trusts has offices within the East Bay Municipal
Utility District.
All of the trusts contract for consultant
services with firms doing business within the East Bay Municipal
Utility District.
-
-
ANALYSIS
-
Income is defined in Section 82030,
in pertinent part,
as any
payment received,
including but not limited to any salary,
wage,
advance,
reimbursement for expenses or per diem.
There are
several exceptions to this broad definition of income.
None of
the exceptions are applicable under the facts you have provided.
2
2
iuong the exceptions are income from an entity that does not
do business within the public official’s jurisdiction.
However,
as the trusts clearly contract for services within the utility
district’s jurisdiction, this exception does not apply.


File No.
A—95—266
Page
3
Accordingly, the reimbursement for expenses and tuition that
Director Mellon receives from the private trusts qualify as income
under the Act and must be reported on Director Mellon’s statement
of economic interests.
If Director Mellon provides equal or
greater consideration to the private trusts through his services,
the payments would not meet the definition of a gift under the
Act.
(Section 82O28.)
If Director Mellon does not provide equal
or greater consideration to the private trusts, gift limits may
apply to these payments.
Should you have any additional questions regarding this
advice request,
you may direct them to the attention of the
undersigned.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
/t
7
.,&_t&
By:
Daniel
. Muallem
Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:DEM:ak
Please note,
however, that pursuant to the definition of
“gift,” any person who claims a payment is not a gift by reason of
receipt of consideration has the burden of proving that the
consideration received is of equal or greater value.
(Section
82028(a).)

not apply to these payments.
